title: ilex tutcheri
intro: Ilex tutcheri, also known as the Tutcher's holly, is a species of flowering plant in the holly family (Aquifoliaceae). This plant is native to the subtropical regions of China and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. The leaves and berries of Ilex tutcheri contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the leaves and berries of Ilex tutcheri are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
